title: aconitum × triste
intro: Aconitum × triste, also known as monk's hood or wolf's bane, is a hybrid plant of the Aconitum genus, known for its toxic and medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems contain aconitine, a powerful neurotoxin, which has been used in herbal medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. Aconitum × triste has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including pain relief, fever reduction, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, powder, or infusion, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine, although caution and proper dosing are essential due to its toxicity.
